title: SICP 4.2 lazy evaluator / normal-order evaluator
goal: Implement the SICP 4.2 lazy evaluator as a runnable Eshkol probe so the full-book SICP gate covers normal-order evaluation, delayed operands, thunk forcing, and memoization under both JIT and AOT.
next_actions: Add tests/sicp/ch4_lazy_evaluator.esk using the existing metacircular evaluator style where practical.
Represent delayed operands as thunks carrying expression and environment.
Implement force-it and actual-value behavior, including memoized thunks if following the memoizing evaluator.
Cover canonical SICP lazy behavior: a branch can avoid forcing an erroring expression, delayed arguments are forced only when needed, and repeated force respects the selected memoization semantics.
Emit clear PASS/FAIL output compatible with scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh.
acceptance: tests/sicp/ch4_lazy_evaluator.esk exists and self-checks the SICP 4.2 lazy evaluator.
The probe passes under AOT compile/run.
ICC sicp-completeness observes sicp_ch4_lazy_evaluator_r and sicp_ch4_lazy_evaluator_aot as PASS.
ux_payoff: Makes normal-order evaluation a real Eshkol capability instead of a documentation claim.
